mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding aggregate demand and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC of PAYEMS level change versus next 20-day returns is negative over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in industrial production below 100 indicates contraction in manufacturing activity, which signals economic weakness and leads to negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: If INDPRO < 100 but SPX forward 5-day return is positive in more than 60% of instances, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales are booming but the Fed is raising rates, tight monetary policy combined with strong consumption creates an unsustainable later-cycle dynamic that depresses forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS YoY growth > 5% and FEDFUNDS increase > 0.5% over 6 months
failure_condition: Both conditions hold but SPX 20-day return is positive in over 50% of triggered observations; reject.

mechanism_narrative: In a rising unemployment regime (UNRATE above its 12-month moving average and increasing), further labor deterioration signals imminent recession risks and produces negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by >0.2% but SPX 60-day return is not statistically lower than the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.10), reject.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation compresses real earnings growth and forces the Fed to tighten, which consistently depresses equity valuations over longer horizons regardless of regime.
failure_condition: Spearman rank correlation between CPIAUCSL level change and next 60-day return is above -0.05 over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.5 million units signal a weakening construction sector and broader demand contraction, leading to negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If HOUST < 1500 is followed by a positive SPX 20-day return in more than 40% of cases, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the economy shows contradictory signals (negative GDP growth alongside positive payroll growth), it indicates structural decoupling that typically resolves with a downturn and negative equity returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth < 0 and PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 0
failure_condition: If the joint condition occurs but SPX 60-day return is negative less than two-thirds of the time, reject.

mechanism_narrative: During a period of industrial supply contraction (index below 100), a monthly rebound in industrial production signals that supply-chain bottlenecks are easing, which boosts equity sentiment over the short term.
failure_condition: In the regime INDPRO < 100, a >1% monthly increase leads to negative SPX 5-day return in over 50% of cases; reject.
